name: You’re Gonna Want To Hear This

episodes.title: Robbed Of Her Childhood: Bek Condello On Escaping A Cult
episodes.description:
What if leaving your faith meant losing everyone you’d ever loved? Author Bek Condello grew up inside the Geelong Revival Centre, one of Australia’s most controlling Pentecostal churches, and in this episode she tells marie claire Australia Editor Georgie McCourt what it really takes to rebuild a life from scratch after you leave a cult. 

#Cult  #ReligiousTrauma  #YoureGonnaWantToHearThis

⚠ CONTENT WARNING: This episode contains themes of childhood abuse, physical punishment, sexual trauma and coercive control. If you or someone you know needs immediate assistance, call 000. For confidential 24/7 crisis support, contact Lifeline on 13 11 14 or https://www.lifeline.org.au. Support is also available through Beyond Blue on 1300 22 4636 or https://www.beyondblue.org.au. 

Raised inside the Geelong Revival Centre, Bek grew up believing the outside world was dangerous. Every aspect of life, from education and friendships to marriage and work, was dictated by church leaders. Curiosity was discouraged, obedience was demanded, and fear shaped almost every decision. 

In this deeply personal conversation, Bek reflects on the childhood she now recognises as abusive, the lasting impact of religious trauma, and the heartbreaking reality that leaving meant losing her family, community and identity. She opens up about purity culture, consent, body shame, marrying to escape one form of control only to enter another, and the years it took to trust herself and build a life on her own terms. 

Today, Bek has transformed unimaginable loss into purpose. As the state of Victoria investigates high-control religious groups, she explains why sharing her story has become about much more than personal healing. It’s about helping others recognise coercion, reclaim their autonomy and know they’re not alone. 

⏱ Chapters

02:13 Realising her childhood wasn’t normal

1:00:06 Where to find Can You Handle a Girl Like Me?
